_doc: Unified compute-fabric EGRESS POLICY — the single source of truth for 'what data may leave this machine, to where'. Enforced by backend/services/egress_guard.js (compute dispatch) and runtime/core/egress_guard.py (LLM provider routing). Deny-by-default + fail-closed: anything not explicitly allowed is BLOCKED. No secrets or hostnames live here — only rules. Both implementations fall back to baked-in defaults if this file is missing/partial so the gate never crashes open.

destination_tiers._doc: Higher rank = more exposure = stricter rules. peer/rented are owned-or-rented infra you control; external_api is a third party (Anthropic/OpenAI/OpenRouter).

policy_matrix._doc: For each destination tier: the action applied to each data classification. block = refuse egress; redact = strip the sensitive spans then send; allow = send as-is. A missing entry defaults to BLOCK (deny-by-default).

endpoint_allow._doc: A worker/provider endpoint URL must match one of these to be dispatchable. Private LAN ranges (paired laptop/PC) + https for rented/cloud. http to public IPs is rejected. Patterns are regex on the full URL.
